metadata.title: Guideline on the reporting of physiologically based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) modelling and simulation

rules.rule_text: If PBPK modelling is intended to support a regulatory decision, the PBPK platform needs to be qualified for the intended use.
rules.verbatim_quote: If PBPK modelling is intended to support a regulatory decision, the PBPK platform needs to be qualified for the intended use.

rules.rule_text: When PBPK platforms are used for regulatory decisions, simulations must be carefully assessed regarding (1) the ability of the platform to adequately perform simulations of the intended type and (2) the predictive performance of the specific drug models.
rules.verbatim_quote: when used for regulatory decisions, simulations performed using PBPK platforms need to be carefully assessed regarding (1) the ability of the platform to adequately perform simulations of the intended type (i.e., the PBPK platform needs to be qualified for the intended use with well characterised in vivo data) (see Appendix 1) and (2) the predictive performance of the specific drug models (see Appendix 2).

rules.rule_text: The submitted PBPK report must include the validity and biological plausibility of input parameters, the uncertainty around the determination or prediction of parameter values, clarity on the model building and optimisation processes, and a discussion of the consequences of the assumptions made.
rules.verbatim_quote: the submitted PBPK report should include the validity and biological plausibility of input parameters, the uncertainty around the determination or prediction of parameter values, clarity on the model building and optimisation processes, and a discussion of the consequences of the assumptions made.
rules.exceptions: The level of evaluations depends on the regulatory impact of the PBPK simulation.

rules.rule_text: If PBPK modelling is used in the development of an investigational drug, it is strongly recommended to use the opportunity to optimally design clinical pharmacology studies that can provide data to progressively improve the model and support the planned model applications.
rules.verbatim_quote: If PBPK modelling is used in the development of an investigational drug, it is strongly recommended to use the opportunity to optimally design clinical pharmacology studies that can provide data to progressively improve the model and support the planned model applications.

rules.rule_text: Seeking CHMP Scientific Advice for additional guidance on the use of PBPK modelling and simulation in support of regulatory decisions is encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: Seeking CHMP Scientific Advice for additional guidance on the use of PBPK modelling and simulation in support of regulatory decisions is encouraged.

rules.rule_text: The objective and the intended regulatory purpose of the PBPK modelling, including any proposed information for inclusion or changes to the Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC), should be clearly described at the start of the report.
rules.verbatim_quote: The objective and the intended regulatory purpose of the PBPK modelling, including any proposed information for inclusion or changes to the Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC), should be clearly described at the start of the report.

rules.rule_text: The introduction of a PBPK report must include the relevant information about the investigational drug relevant for the model, including physicochemical properties, in vivo and in vitro ADME, and for therapeutic proteins, specific parameters such as extravasation, FcRn recycling, and target mediated disposition.
rules.verbatim_quote: The introduction of a PBPK report should include the relevant information about the investigational drug relevant for the model, like physicochemical properties. This should emphasise in vivo and in vitro ADME, or, if applicable, therapeutic protein specific parameters, like extravasation, FcRn recycling, and target mediated disposition and other pharmacokinetic parameters of the drug.

rules.rule_text: A summary of clinical studies must be included in the PBPK report. The data included should be related to the intended purpose of the model.
rules.verbatim_quote: A summary of clinical studies should be included. The data included should be related to the intended purpose of the model.

rules.rule_text: A quantitative mass-balance diagram presenting elimination pathways with involved enzymes and transporters must be included along with explanatory text and references.
rules.verbatim_quote: A quantitative mass-balance diagram (Figure 1) presenting elimination pathways with involved enzymes and transporters should be included along with explanatory text and references.

rules.rule_text: For biopharmaceutical applications, a full description of drug substance and product properties must be provided, including particle size, form, solubility and dissolution data at physiologically relevant pH values and media.
rules.verbatim_quote: For biopharmaceutical applications, a full description of drug substance and product properties is of importance e.g. particle size, form, solubility and dissolution data at physiological relevant pH values and media.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric applications, an overview of available pharmacokinetic information in other age groups must be presented, and effects of ontogeny such as potential quantitative changes in the contributions of the various elimination pathways in paediatric age subsets must be addressed.
rules.verbatim_quote: For paediatric applications an overview of available pharmacokinetic information in other age groups, such as older children and adults, should be presented. Effects of ontogeny such as potential quantitative changes in the contributions of the various elimination pathways in paediatric age subsets should be addressed.

rules.rule_text: The PBPK report should include sufficient background information to place the PBPK modelling in context in the clinical development of the drug, including a summary of available knowledge about the exposure-response relationship for efficacy and safety and/or the exposure level at the therapeutic dose in the pivotal efficacy/safety trial population. If possible, a well justified target plasma exposure should be defined.
rules.verbatim_quote: The report should include sufficient background information to place the PBPK modelling in context in the clinical development of the drug. The background information should also contain a summary of the available knowledge about the exposure-response relationship for efficacy and safety and/or the exposure level at the therapeutic dose in the pivotal efficacy/safety trial population. If possible, a well justified target plasma exposure (a range for relevant exposure parameters specifying what change in exposure would justify a posology adjustment) should be defined.

rules.rule_text: The qualification of the platform for its intended purpose must be presented and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The qualification of the platform for its intended purpose should be presented and justified.
rules.exceptions: Experience of qualification of models is limited at the current time however some recommendations with examples are detailed in Appendix 1.

rules.rule_text: Where a CHMP qualification opinion or advice has been obtained, a link to this on the EMA website must be submitted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a CHMP qualification opinion or advice has been obtained, a link to this on the EMA website should be submitted.

rules.rule_text: If commercial software is concerned, the name and version of the PBPK platform used must be stated and any subsequent modification clearly documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: If commercial software is concerned, the name and version of PBPK platform used should be stated and any subsequent modification clearly documented.

rules.rule_text: An explicit and systematic discussion of the assumptions made in the submitted system and drug model and in the associated analysis must be provided. Data to support the assumptions and their biological and/or pharmacological rationale must be presented and discussed, as well as the impact the assumptions have on the model and the outcome.
rules.verbatim_quote: An explicit and systematic discussion of the assumptions made in the submitted system and drug model and in the associated analysis should be provided. Data to support the assumptions and their biological and/or pharmacological rationale should be presented and discussed, as well as the impact the assumptions have on the model and the outcome.

rules.rule_text: The approaches used to test the assumptions and the outcomes must be presented.
rules.verbatim_quote: The approaches used to test the assumptions and the outcomes should be presented.

rules.rule_text: The system-dependent parameters, including physiological parameters for the population(s) for which qualification is claimed, must be presented and justified. Any modification of default values of system-dependent parameters of the dataset must be highlighted. Literature references must be provided as full articles and the rationale for the chosen system-dependent parameter values must be given. The data must be presented in an appendix to the report in a structured way to allow assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: The system-dependent parameters, including physiological parameters for the population(s) for which qualification is claimed, should be presented and justified. Any modification of default values of system-dependent parameters of the dataset should be highlighted. Literature references should be provided as full articles and the rationale for the chosen system-dependent parameter values should be given. The data should be presented in an appendix to the report in a structured way to allow assessment.
rules.exceptions: For paediatric modelling the effect of ontogeny and allometry on system values could be justified using a worst case approach supported by peer reviewed references.

rules.rule_text: Any modification of the default values of system-dependent parameters of the dataset must be summarised and justified, including changes to values of the degradation constant (Kdeg) of metabolising enzymes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any modification of the default values of system-dependent parameters of the dataset should be summarised and justified e.g., changing the values of the degradation constant (kdeg) of metabolising enzymes (Guideline on investigation of drug interactions, CPMP/EWP/560/95/Rev. 1).

rules.rule_text: The PBPK report must include a thorough description of the drug model structure and drug-dependent parameters.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The PBPK report should include a thorough description of the drug model structure and drug-dependent parameters.

rules.rule_text: A summary of the drug-specific parameter names and values (mean with known or predicted variability: SD or range [min-max]), and the sources of the values must be included in a tabular format. The value of the drug-specific parameter must particularly be justified in the text.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: A summary of the drug-specific parameter names and values (mean with known or predicted variability: SD or range [min-max]), and the sources of the values should be included in a tabular format. The value of the drug-specific parameter should particularly be justified in the text.

rules.rule_text: The parameters described must include physicochemical properties and ADME data that were used to parameterise the model. If there is more than one source of a parameter with notably different values, the value chosen must be justified and the consequences discussed.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The parameters described should include physicochemical properties and ADME data that were used to parameterise the model. If there is more than one source of a parameter with notably different values, the value chosen should be justified and the consequences discussed.

rules.rule_text: Some parameters in the model can be either experimental or predicted (e.g. fumic, log D). If there is a range, the dependence of the model for such parameters must be assessed. If such a parameter is considered important for the model it should preferably be measured or otherwise justified as to why this is not possible, or the predicted value is considered more accurate.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: Some parameters in the model can be either experimental or predicted (e.g. fumic, log D). If there is a range, the dependence of the model for such parameters should be assessed. If such a parameter is considered important for the model it should preferably be measured or otherwise justified as to why this is not possible, or the predicted value is considered more accurate.

rules.rule_text: For estimated parameters, the chosen estimation method/procedure must be described, such as the used objective function, minimisation method and error models. Estimated parameter values must also be discussed with regard to their range of known values, biological plausibility and precision of estimation.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: For estimated parameters, the chosen estimation method/procedure must be described, such as the used objective function, minimisation method and error models. Estimated parameter values should also be discussed with regard to their range of known values, biological plausibility and precision of estimation.

rules.rule_text: Consideration must be given as to whether there are drug specific parameters in the model that are correlated. If more than one of the unknown parameters are estimated through fitting of the model using observed clinical data, identifiability issues must be addressed. A description on how any identifiability issues have been handled must be given.
rules.source_section: 4.4.3. Drug parameters and the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: Consideration should be given as to whether there are drug specific parameters in the model that are correlated (such as Ki and kinact). If more than one of the unknown parameters are estimated through fitting of the model using observed clinical data, there is a risk that these parameters cannot be uniquely identifiable. In the case that an identifiability issue is suspected, additional in vitro or clinical data may be required to increase certainty, on one or both of the parameters. A description on how any identifiability issues have been handled should be given.

rules.rule_text: The use of an analysis plan is recommended. An overview of the model building process must be submitted. Any adaptation of the model to optimise the fit of the simulation to in vivo results must be described with respect to its rationale and outcome.
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of an analysis plan is recommended. An overview of the model building process should be submitted. any adaptation of the model to optimise the fit of the simulation to in vivo results should be described with respect to its rationale and outcome.

rules.rule_text: A description of the design of the study to be simulated as well as the target virtual population must be provided. This must include, but not be limited to, dosing information, length of study, number of individuals and characteristics of the virtual population. The appropriateness of the virtual population must be justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the design of the study to be simulated as well as the target virtual population should be provided. This should include, but not be limited to, dosing information, length of study, number of individuals and characteristics of the virtual population. The appropriateness of the virtual population should be justified.

rules.rule_text: A comprehensive summary of the system and drug model evaluation must be provided. An evaluation of the model must be presented in sufficient detail in the report to support confidence for regulators in the application of the model in their decision making.
rules.verbatim_quote: A comprehensive summary of the system and drug model evaluation should be provided. A thorough evaluation of the drug model is important if the model is to be used to simulate novel situations, e.g. a drug interaction or pharmacokinetics in an alternate population. An evaluation of the model should be presented in sufficient detail in the report to support confidence for regulators in the application of the model in their decision making (Appendix 2).

rules.rule_text: Sensitivity analysis must be undertaken for parameters that are considered to be key to the model or that are uncertain. The range of the parameter values to be tested must be pre-specified and the range must be justified based on scientific rationale, published data or known variability in the estimate.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sensitivity analysis should be undertaken for parameters that are considered to be key to the model (i.e. that are likely to markedly influence the outcome) or parameters that are considered uncertain. ... The range of the parameter values to be tested should be pre-specified and the range should be justified based on scientific rationale, published data or known variability in the estimate.
rules.exceptions: An alternative approach could involve the use of an integrated population PBPK approach where uncertainty of parameters and known variability is incorporated in the model.

rules.rule_text: A 'worst-case' approach is recommended for sensitivity analysis, e.g. for CYP enzymes 10-fold, for transporters 30-fold. To allow for assessment of the impact of correlated parameters, a global approach may be performed. Otherwise a joint sensitivity analysis, where two or more parameters are tested simultaneously, may be the preferred choice.
rules.verbatim_quote: A 'worst-case' approach is recommended e.g. for CYP enzymes 10-fold, for transporters 30-fold. To allow for assessment of the impact of correlated parameters, a global approach may be performed. Otherwise a joint sensitivity analysis, where two or more parameters are tested simultaneously, may be the preferred choice.

rules.rule_text: Sensitivity analysis must be performed both during the development and application of the drug model. For PBPK models of investigational drug when used to predict inhibition of an enzyme, sensitivity analysis should be performed on the interaction parameter Ki. For PBPK models where the absorption is of importance, parameters which may impact drug absorption should be subjected to sensitivity analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sensitivity analysis should be performed both during the development and application of the drug model (Figure 2). For example, for PBPK models of investigational drug when used to predict inhibition of an enzyme, sensitivity analysis should be performed on the interaction parameter Ki (Guideline on investigation of drug interactions CPMP/EWP/560/95/Rev. 1). For PBPK models where the absorption is of importance, parameters which may impact drug absorption should be subjected to sensitivity analysis such as gastrointestinal pH, particle size and other parameters that are uncertain or that cannot be measured such as unbound concentration in the enterocyte.

rules.rule_text: When PBPK is used for simulation in the paediatric population, additional sensitivity analysis related to ontogeny of enzymes and transporters involved must be performed, if relevant.
rules.verbatim_quote: When PBPK is used for simulation in the paediatric population additional sensitivity analysis related to ontogeny of enzymes and transporters involved should be performed, if relevant.

rules.rule_text: The drug model must be shown to be capable of predicting the observed pharmacokinetics of the compound before the model can be used for simulations of special situations.
rules.source_section: 4.7.2. Evaluation of the predictive performance of the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The drug model must be shown to be capable of predicting the observed pharmacokinetics of the compound before the model can be used for simulations of special situations.

rules.rule_text: The results of the model evaluation and final simulation(s) must be presented in a clear and comprehensive manner. The relevant simulated pharmacokinetic parameters must be tabulated and presented visually using figures and graphs. The parameter values must be reported with descriptive statistics such as geometric mean and standard deviation and/or range.
rules.verbatim_quote: The results of the model evaluation and final simulation(s) should be presented in a clear and comprehensive manner. The relevant simulated pharmacokinetic parameters (e.g., AUC, Cmax, t1/2, Cmin, V, CL, accumulation / interaction ratios, and inter-individual variability) should be tabulated and presented visually using figures and graphs. The parameter values should be reported with descriptive statistics such as geometric mean and standard deviation and/or range.

rules.rule_text: The relevant model files that were used to generate the final PBPK simulations (including compound and population files) must be provided in a tabular format in the report as well as submitted electronically separately in an executable format.
rules.verbatim_quote: The relevant model files that were used to generate the final PBPK simulations (including compound and population files) should be provided in a tabular format in the report as well as submitted electronically separately in an executable format.

rules.rule_text: The outcome of performed sensitivity analysis must be provided in the results section.
rules.verbatim_quote: The outcome of performed sensitivity analysis should be provided (see Section 4.7.1.).

rules.rule_text: The contribution of the PBPK modelling and simulations to the regulatory decision making and the regulatory use must be explicitly stated. The confidence in the model predictions must be considered before conclusions are drawn based on the model, and it must be discussed how the potential uncertainty may influence the decision making.
rules.verbatim_quote: The contribution of the PBPK modelling and simulations to the regulatory decision making and the regulatory use should be explicitly stated. The confidence in the model predictions should be considered before conclusions are drawn based on the model, and it should be discussed how the potential uncertainty may influence the decision making.

rules.rule_text: To certify that a PBPK platform can be used for an intended regulatory purpose, the ability of the platform to perform that specific type of simulation should be evaluated and, in some cases, this requires that the PBPK platform should be qualified for the intended purpose. The extent of qualification required depends on the regulatory impact of the modelling.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification of the PBPK platform
rules.verbatim_quote: To certify that a PBPK platform can be used for an intended regulatory purpose, the ability of the platform to perform that specific type of simulation should be evaluated and in some cases, this requires that the PBPK platform should be qualified for the intended purpose. The extent of qualification required depends on the regulatory impact of the modelling (see below).

rules.rule_text: A qualification issued within the context of a particular regulatory submission should be considered only valid for that particular submission and would need to be resubmitted and re-evaluated in future applications.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification of the PBPK platform
rules.verbatim_quote: The qualification could also be assessed within the context of a regulatory submission. However, a qualification issued within the context of a particular regulatory submission should be considered only valid for that particular submission and would need to be resubmitted and re-evaluated in future applications.
rules.exceptions: If there is a CHMP qualification opinion supporting the intended use of the platform, a reference to the EMA web site is sufficient and the qualification can be referred to in future applications with the same intended use.

rules.rule_text: In general, to qualify the system model of a PBPK platform, compounds with similar ADME characteristics to that of the intended use should be included in a pre-specified data set. For each drug compound, in vivo and in vitro data relevant for the intended use should be supplied in a table form. It is considered that eight to ten compounds is indicative of a sufficient number.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification of the PBPK platform for the intended purpose
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, to qualify the system model of a PBPK platform, compounds with similar ADME characteristics to that of the intended use should be included in a pre-specified data set. For each drug compound, in vivo and in vitro data relevant for the intended use should be supplied in a table form. ... It is recognised that the qualification dataset is data, and application, specific and therefore needs to be justified in each case. It is considered that e.g. eight to ten compounds is indicative of a sufficient number.
rules.exceptions: The number is application specific and needs to be justified in each case.

rules.rule_text: If possible, it should be ensured that there are additional drugs included in the qualification set that were not used in the platform building.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification of the PBPK platform for the intended purpose
rules.verbatim_quote: If possible, it should be ensured that there are additional drugs included in the qualification set that were not used in the platform building.

rules.rule_text: In case of doubt on the relevance or the robustness of available system data included in the platform, particularly if used for high regulatory impact simulations, the applicant is strongly encouraged to seek CHMP Scientific Advice for further guidance.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification of the PBPK platform for the intended purpose
rules.verbatim_quote: 'in case of doubt on the relevance or the robustness of available system data included in the platform, particularly if used for high regulatory impact simulations, the applicant is strongly encouraged to seek CHMP Scientific Advice for further guidance.

rules.rule_text: The regulatory impact of modelling must be discussed and justified in the submission. High impact analyses include: use of PBPK model in place of clinical data to waive interaction studies or simulate non-studied scenarios; evaluation of the investigational drug as a victim of DDIs in a pharmacogenetic subpopulation, or in paediatric patients; evaluation of complex DDIs such as combined effect of two inhibitors; and prediction of DDI assessing other posologies compared to an available DDI study.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Qualification requirements at different levels of regulatory impact / High regulatory impact analyses
rules.verbatim_quote: The level of regulatory impact should be discussed and justified in the submission. ... High impact simulations could include but are not limited to: the use of a PBPK model in place of clinical data (e.g. to waive interaction studies, to simulate non-studied scenarios); evaluation of the investigational drug as a victim of DDIs in a pharmacogenetic subpopulation, or in paediatric patients; evaluation of complex DDIs where e.g. the combined effect of two inhibitors are simulated; prediction of drug-drug interaction assessing other posologies compared to an available DDI study.

rules.rule_text: To qualify the PBPK platform to quantitatively predict the effect of inhibition of an enzyme on the pharmacokinetics of drugs metabolised by the enzyme, adequate prediction of observed in vivo effects of inhibition of the enzyme in question must be demonstrated using a pre-specified qualification dataset, and must include simulation of inhibition effects on drug exposure and derived pharmacokinetic parameters such as total clearance, clearance through each pathway, bioavailability, AUC, Cmax, t1/2.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 1 — Qualification of the ability to quantify the effects on investigational drugs being victim of drug interaction
rules.verbatim_quote: To qualify the PBPK platform to quantitatively predict the effect of inhibition of an enzyme on the pharmacokinetics of drugs metabolised by the enzyme, adequate prediction of observed in vivo effects of inhibition of the enzyme in question should be demonstrated. This should be made using a pre-specified qualification dataset and should include simulation of inhibition effects on drug exposure and derived pharmacokinetic parameters such as total clearance, clearance through each pathway, bioavailability, AUC, Cmax, t1/2 etc.
rules.exceptions: If the Applicant wants to qualify the platform for inhibition processes that are time-dependent, additional parameters should be simulated, such as time to steady state.

rules.rule_text: The qualification dataset for DDI victim drug should, if possible, consist of a series of drug substances (victims) eliminated to a significant extent through metabolism catalysed by the enzyme in question. For each drug, in vivo data supporting the clearance fraction of the pathway/contribution of the enzyme (fm) must be presented. Preferably, the chosen drug substances should reflect different degrees of dependence of clearance on blood flow, plasma protein binding and, if relevant, different degrees of intestinal first-pass metabolism.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 1 — Qualification of the ability to quantify the effects on investigational drugs being victim of drug interaction
rules.verbatim_quote: The qualification dataset should, if possible, consist of a series of drug substances (victims) eliminated to a significant extent through metabolism catalysed by the enzyme in question. For each drug, in vivo data supporting the clearance fraction of the pathway/contribution of the enzyme (fm) should be presented. Preferably, the chosen drug substances should reflect different degrees of dependence of clearance on blood flow, plasma protein binding and, if relevant, different degrees of intestinal first-pass metabolism.

rules.rule_text: For qualification of the platform's ability to detect investigational drugs as perpetrators of drug interaction, the qualification must aim to show the capacity to detect the observed in vivo inhibitory effect of different inhibitors on sensitive probe substrate(s) for the enzyme in question and the ability to quantitatively predict available in vivo DDI study results. The qualification dataset should be pre-specified and should include inhibitors of different potency. The fm of the substrate should be confirmed by in vivo data.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 2 — Qualification of the ability to detect investigational drugs as perpetrators of drug interaction
rules.verbatim_quote: The qualification should aim at showing the capacity to detect the observed in vivo inhibitory effect of different inhibitors on sensitive probe substrate(s) for the enzyme in question and the ability to quantitatively predict available in vivo DDI study results need to be shown. The qualification dataset should be pre-specified and should include inhibitors of different potency. The predictive performance of the probe substrate PBPK model included in the qualification should be demonstrated (see appendix 2). Furthermore, the fm of the substrate should be confirmed by in vivo data, e.g. from a study with a selective strong inhibitor of the enzyme or from a study in a genetic sub-population having a markedly reduced activity of the enzyme.
rules.exceptions: Non-clinical data may only be used for fm estimation e.g. at an early stage of model development or for a low impact example.

rules.rule_text: If the enzyme is expressed at multiple sites, accurate prediction of inhibition at each site must be demonstrated using data for probe drugs with well characterised interactions at each site and with focus on PK parameters where data exists.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 2 — Qualification of the ability to detect investigational drugs as perpetrators of drug interaction
rules.verbatim_quote: If the enzyme, such as CYP3A4, is expressed at multiple sites, accurate prediction of inhibition at each site should be demonstrated using data for probe drugs with well characterised interactions at each site and with focus on PK parameters (e.g. Cmax, AUC), where data exists. The inhibition at the site of the enzyme over time should be discussed, if this were for a CYP3A substrate, it would be considered adequate to separate the intestinal and hepatic contributions due to their differential effects on Cmax vs t1/2.

rules.rule_text: The qualification for DDI perpetrator will only be valid for situations covered by the qualification dataset, e.g. only for the specific enzyme(s), site of inhibition (e.g. liver, intestine) and the type of background data (including pharmacokinetic data, the system parameters and the population used) on which the simulations were based.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 2 — Qualification of the ability to detect investigational drugs as perpetrators of drug interaction
rules.verbatim_quote: The qualification will only be valid for situations covered by the qualification dataset, e.g. only for the specific enzyme(s), site of inhibition (e.g. liver, intestine) and the type of background data (including pharmacokinetic data, the system parameters and the population used) on which the simulations were based.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric PBPK modelling without clinical PK data in the target age range, the platform qualification must meet strict qualification requirements including predictive performance of the model for a large number of compounds with similar ADME properties in the same population.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Example 3 — Simulation of exposure in paediatric population
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of PBPK modelling without clinical PK data in the target age range, the platform qualification needs to meet strict qualification requirements including predictive performance of the model for a large number of compounds with similar ADME properties in the same population.
rules.exceptions: Posology recommendations in children that are supported by only limited, or no, clinical exposure data and heavily rely on PBPK modelling are considered to be of high regulatory impact applications.

rules.rule_text: The predictive performance of any compound files (e.g. inhibitors, inducers and probe drugs) used in a simulation needs to be confirmed. The simulated pharmacokinetics of the specific drug included in the file should be compared against several representative in vivo pharmacokinetic studies for this drug. The data to be supplied includes AUC, Cmax, t1/2 and the plasma concentration-time course including the shape (both linear and semi-log graphs).
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Compound files supplied in the PBPK platform
rules.verbatim_quote: The predictive performance of any compound files (e.g. inhibitors, inducers and probe drugs) used in a simulation needs to be confirmed. This could be done in a qualification procedure for an intended purpose of the PBPK platform or in a regulatory submission (section 1). To support that a compound file can be used for simulation, the simulated pharmacokinetics of the specific drug included in the file should be compared against several representative in vivo pharmacokinetic studies for this drug. The data to be supplied includes AUC, Cmax, t1/2 and the plasma concentration-time course including the shape (both linear and semi-log graphs).

rules.rule_text: Differences between PBPK platform versions must be clearly stated in the report and discussed. If a given version of a platform has previously been considered qualified for a certain use, the possibility to extrapolate the qualification from the previous version to the updated new version(s) must be justified if the new version is to be used for a regulatory purpose.
rules.source_section: Appendix 1: Compound files supplied in the PBPK platform
rules.verbatim_quote: Differences between PBPK platform versions should be clearly stated in the report and discussed. If a given version of a platform has previously been considered qualified for a certain use, the possibility to extrapolate the qualification from the previous version to the updated new version(s) should be justified if the new version is to be used for a regulatory purpose.

rules.rule_text: Details of the differential equations used and the parameterisations of the PBPK model need to be presented for verification. The maintenance of mass-balance as well as blood flow balances within the model must be supported. It must be ensured that there are no numerical errors. Verification results must be available on request.
rules.verbatim_quote: Details of the differential equations used and the parameterisations of the PBPK model needs to be presented. The maintenance of mass-balance as well as blood flow balances within the model should be supported. It should be ensured that there are no numerical errors. If the PBPK platform has gone through a CHMP qualification procedure for an intended purpose, it is assumed that the verification is satisfactory for the parts of the platform used for this purpose. In other cases, the verification approach that has been used to support the PBPK platform as well as the verification results should be available on request.
rules.exceptions: If the PBPK platform has gone through a CHMP qualification procedure for an intended purpose, it is assumed that the verification is satisfactory for the parts of the platform used for this purpose.

rules.rule_text: The PBPK report must include an evaluation of the investigational drug PBPK model to ensure that the drug model consistently describes the observed pharmacokinetic behaviour of the drug. The evaluation must be made by assessing the ability of the model to predict the range of the observed outcome of representative in vivo pharmacokinetic studies or population pharmacokinetic analyses, e.g. different dose levels and single and repeated drug administrations. Simulations should be performed for a large number of individuals (>100).
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Evaluation of the predictive performance of the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The PBPK report should include an evaluation of the investigational drug PBPK model, to ensure that the drug model consistently describes the observed pharmacokinetic behaviour of the drug. The evaluation should be made by assessing the ability of the model to predict the range of the observed outcome of representative in vivo pharmacokinetic studies or population pharmacokinetic analyses, e.g. different dose levels and single and repeated drug administrations. Simulations should be performed in the population of interest and for a large number of individuals (>100) is recommended.
rules.exceptions: Additional support could be gained by simulating potential dose dependency (non-linearity), drug interactions different routes of administration and urine excretion.

rules.rule_text: The comparison of the simulated and the observed plasma concentration-time data must be presented as plots, such as simulated vs. observed data using linear and semi-log plots and as tabulations. Observed individual data must be used for comparison. Visual predictive plots must be presented comparing the central trend and variability of the observed data with the simulation, including both log and linear scale with observed percentiles (5th and 95th) of the observations and the simulation-based confidence intervals (i.e. 95%) around the observed percentile.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Evaluation of the predictive performance of the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The comparison of the simulated and the observed plasma concentration-time data should be presented as plots, such as simulated vs. observed data using linear and semi-log plots and as tabulations. Observed individual data should be used for comparison. Visual predictive plots should be presented comparing the central trend and variability of the observed data with the simulation (both log and linear scale including observed percentiles (i.e. 5th and 95th) of the observations and the simulation-based confidence intervals (i.e. 95%) around the observed percentile).

rules.rule_text: The consequences of poor prediction in any part of the plasma concentration time curve must be discussed and justified (Cmax, tmax, t1/2 and AUC). The evaluation of the drug model for a certain purpose should focus on evaluating the parts of the drug model that are central to the intended purpose.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Evaluation of the predictive performance of the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The consequences of poor prediction in any part of the plasma concentration time curve should be discussed and justified (Cmax, tmax, t1/2 and AUC). The evaluation of the drug model for a certain purpose should focus on evaluating the parts of the drug model that are central to the intended purpose.

rules.rule_text: The acceptance criteria (adequacy of prediction) for the closeness of the comparison of simulated and observed data depends on the regulatory impact and needs to be considered separately for each application. The consequences of poor prediction must be discussed and justified, e.g. the acceptance limits for a victim drug must be set in perspective of the concentration-effect and concentration-safety relationships of the drug at the time of submission. Biologically plausible reasons for any discrepancy in the prediction must also be considered.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Evaluation of the predictive performance of the drug model
rules.verbatim_quote: The acceptance criteria (adequacy of prediction) for the closeness of the comparison of simulated and observed data depends on the regulatory impact and needs to be considered separately for each application. The consequences of poor prediction should be discussed and justified, e.g. the acceptance limits for a victim drug must be set in perspective of the concentration-effect and concentration-safety relationships of the drug at the time of submission. Biologically plausible reasons for any discrepancy in the prediction should also be considered.

rules.rule_text: When the investigational drug is a victim of a DDI, the prediction of basic in vivo pharmacokinetic data must be presented. If the interaction is mediated via a metabolic enzyme, predictions of the results from an in vivo drug-interaction study with a known strong inhibitor of that enzyme must be demonstrated to confirm the contribution of the metabolic pathway to the elimination of the drug. If a polymorphic enzyme is involved in the metabolism, adequate prediction of the results of a study on the effects of pharmacogenetics could be used to confirm the accuracy of the drug model.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Example 1 — Evaluation of the drug model when the investigational drug is a victim drug
rules.verbatim_quote: When the investigational drug is a victim of a DDI, the prediction of basic in vivo pharmacokinetic data should be presented. If the interaction is mediated via a metabolic enzyme, predictions of the results from an in vivo drug-interaction study with a known strong inhibitor of that enzyme should be demonstrated to confirm the contribution of the metabolic pathway to the elimination of the drug. If a polymorphic enzyme is involved in the metabolism, adequate prediction of the results of a study on the effects of pharmacogenetics could be used to confirm the accuracy of the drug model.

rules.rule_text: When assessing the simulated results of the DDI for a victim drug, it must be considered whether the inhibitor used in the study may affect other processes determining the disposition of the investigational drug. If the enzyme mediating the interaction is significantly present in both the intestine and liver, the absorption of the investigational drug must be well captured by the PBPK model and adequate prediction of effects on the investigational drug must be shown for inhibition at both locations with satisfactory prediction of Cmax and t1/2 as well as AUC.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Example 1 — Evaluation of the drug model when the investigational drug is a victim drug
rules.verbatim_quote: When assessing the simulated results of the drug-drug interaction, it should be considered whether the inhibitor used in the study may affect other processes determining the disposition of the investigational drug (such as other metabolic enzymes and transport proteins). Also, if the enzyme mediating the interaction is significantly present in both the intestine and liver, it is important that the absorption of the investigational drug is well captured by the PBPK model and adequate prediction of effects on the investigational drug needs to be shown for inhibition at both locations with satisfactory prediction of Cmax and t1/2 as well as AUC.

rules.rule_text: For simulations aiming at qualitatively predicting the in vivo relevance of an observed in vitro enzyme inhibition by the investigational drug, the unbound concentration at the site of the enzyme must be adequately simulated, supported by adequate prediction of the plasma concentration-time course for the investigational drug including relevant PK parameters such as AUC, Cmax and t1/2. If relevant, the possibility of transporter effects leading to higher hepatocyte than blood concentrations must be considered in the model.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Example 2 — Evaluation of the drug model when the investigational drug is a perpetrator
rules.verbatim_quote: For simulations aiming at qualitatively predicting the in vivo relevance of an observed in vitro enzyme inhibition by the investigational drug, it is important that the unbound concentration at the site of the enzyme is adequately simulated. This is supported by adequate prediction of the plasma concentration-time course for the investigational drug including relevant PK parameters such as AUC, Cmax and t1/2. If relevant, the possibility of transporter effects leading to higher hepatocyte than blood concentrations needs to be considered in the model (Guideline on investigation of drug interactions CPMP/EWP/560/95/Rev. 1).

rules.rule_text: If the enzyme is present in the intestine, adequate prediction of the absorption of the investigational drug must be demonstrated.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Example 2 — Evaluation of the drug model when the investigational drug is a perpetrator
rules.verbatim_quote: If the enzyme is present in the intestine, adequate prediction of the absorption of the investigational drug should be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: When the investigational drug is to be simulated in a new population, the drug model evaluation must include demonstration of adequate prediction of the observed exposure in other populations; e.g., in paediatric applications, simulations of the pharmacokinetics in adults and other paediatric age groups can be used as supportive data.
rules.source_section: Appendix 2: Example 3 — Simulation of exposure in an alternate population
rules.verbatim_quote: When the investigational drug is to be simulated in a new population, the drug model evaluation should include demonstration of adequate prediction of the observed exposure in other populations; e.g., in paediatric applications, simulations of the pharmacokinetics in adults and other paediatric age groups can be used as supportive data.
